content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 15, 1976


Cerrell Associates, Inc.
Messrs. Joseph L. and Lee Cerrell
5967 West 3rd Street, Suite 302
Los Angeles, CA. 90036

Gentlemen:

     Thank you for your letter of March 18, 1976, requesting
advice relative to whether the activities which you describe
require you to file a campaign statement as a committee.
When you make a campaign contribution on behalf of a client,
you are not, yourselves, making a contribution within the
meaning of the Political Reform Act and you, therefore, would
not, by reason of this activity alone, become a committee.
The operative section of the Political Reform Act with which
you should be concerned is Gov. Code §84302 which prohibits
making a contribution on behalf of another unless certain
requirements are adhered to.  I will discuss this matter with
representatives of the Secretary of State's office so that,
in the future, the advice of the two agencies with respect to
this issue will be consistent.

     If you have further questions, please do not hesitate to
call me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth Pinney,
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division
KF:pvp


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT-CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

The image appears to be mostly blank or contains extremely faint text that is not legible enough to transcribe accurately. I can see what appear to be two small circular marks or holes near the top of the page, but no clearly readable text content.

FPPC
Mar 29 6:57 AM '76

March 18, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street
Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Cerrell Associates, Inc. is a public relations and campaign management
firm.

We occasionally give small donations from Cerrell Associates to candidates.
We also on occasion advance the money for a contribution on behalf of
a client. That is, our client, XYZ Co. might call and say, "Please get
us a couple of tickets for the John Smith Dinner." We would then write
out Cerrell Associates company check to John Smith's campaign committee
noting on the check that this was a contribution on behalf of XYZ Company.
On the first of the next month our statement to the XYZ Company would
show, in addition to the regular fee and various other expenses, an
expense item for "2 tickets for John Smith Dinner - $200." This would
then be paid in their regular monthly check to us.

We previously reported as a major donor although since it wasn't our
money we weren't sure we should even report. The client, of course,
files as a major donor if their contribution exceeds $5000. After
talking to the Secretary of State's office, they advised that we must
register as a Committee. We did this. After completing the Committee
report for 1975, we definitely do not feel that we should be a Committee.

We respectfully request that you advise us of our reporting requirements.

Sincerely,

CERRELL ASSOCIATES, INC.

Joseph R. Cerrell, President

Lee Cerrell, Treasurer


JRC:cm

432

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with only some very faint marks or shadows visible at the top and what appears to be a page break or line in the middle-lower portion. No clearly legible text is visible to transcribe.]
